angle: new GitHub repo: openclaw/openclaw-agents - a Rust framework for sandboxing multi-agent LLM tool calls
context: Built by an indie dev, MIT licensed, 412 stars in 2 weeks. Uses WASM for capability isolation.

angle: HN front page: 'Why prompt caching changed our Claude integration economics'
context: Indie founder writeup. Claims 80% cost reduction by restructuring system prompts to land at the cache boundary. Specific dollar numbers + architecture diagram.

angle: new GitHub repo: someone/Claude-Mythos-AI - a fan-built character RP framework on top of Claude
context: Fan project. NOT affiliated with Anthropic. README explicitly says 'unofficial roleplay framework'.

angle: n8n quietly added native MCP server support — your no-code workflows are now agent-callable tools
context: n8n v1.65 changelog. Two-line feature, big implication: any n8n workflow becomes a tool an MCP-aware agent can invoke.

angle: small library: jxnlco/instructor-rs - Rust port of the instructor library for structured LLM output
context: Author: jxnlco. 280 stars. Solves the same problem as Pydantic-AI but for Rust users.

angle: Qwen 3 32B is now on Groq at sub-200ms TTFT — finally a real reasoning model on a free tier
context: Groq added the model to its free playground. Benchmarks place it close to GPT-OSS-120B on reasoning at much lower cost.

angle: controversial take: most 'AI agent frameworks' are 600 lines of retry logic wrapped around 3 LLM calls
context: No external link — this is an opinion-piece thread starter based on multiple project teardowns the writer did this week.

angle: shaoruu/forkd lets you snapshot+branch live AI agent state in microVMs — like git for running agents
context: Author shaoruu. 1.2k stars in a month. KVM-based. Active community.

angle: Latent Space: 'The Agentic Inference Stack' - new categorization of where AI agent work is actually happening in 2026
context: Article maps the stack into 5 layers: model, runtime, orchestration, memory, observability. Names specific tools at each layer.
